feature_name: v1.24 "UX Round" -- a single branch (feature/v1.24-ux-round, off main at v1.23.10) bundling a LARGE set of user-experience improvements Dean gathered from real usage: the 9 briefed FRs PLUS all remaining UX items from ROADMAP.md's Planned sections (Player/mobile UX, Mobile polish, Downloads/yt-dlp, Library & discovery, Visual polish). PLAN-GATED /kickoff-complex: a full grounded EXECUTION LIST (Discovery -> Design -> task breakdown / exec plan with a proposed build order in collision-free waves) is produced FIRST for Dean's approval BEFORE any implementation. Coordinator owns ALL git; SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint, never commit.

wave_status.W1: shipped as v1.24.0 (Wave 1 — visual + library quick wins, T1-T5)
wave_status.W2: shipped as v1.24.1 (Wave 2 — Subscriptions, T6, T7, T8)
wave_status.W3: shipped as v1.24.2 (Wave 3 — Library data: T9 move+re-key, T10 stats+viewCount, T11 date/avatar capture; + move-trigger, viewCount-ping, C6 pin-avatar enrichment, subscription-DnD CSS, and gate fixes). Two-reviewer gate clean; 2076 tests green.

wave_plan.note: 7 collision-free waves, T1-T19. One-owner-per-hot-file-per-wave invariant verified; serialize-after markers where two same-wave tasks share a file. EM refined the PE table's under-specified CLIENT-side ownership in Waves 2/3/5 (see exec-plan ## Task breakdown 'EM refinements' subsection). Coordinator owns ALL git; SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint only.

pe_design.note: PRINCIPAL-ENGINEER completed Design. Wrote the ## Design section into docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-09-v1.24-ux-round.md, grounded against live code (url.js/args.js/run.js/store.js/index.js/activity.js, server.js delete+scan+getMediaId, common.js DnD/sortItems, player.js resume/dock, watch.js commentBank/avatar, four-shell favicon). Resolved all 7 PE design questions. Ready for EM task breakdown.
pe_design.critical_hazard: getMediaId(filePath) = md5(filePath): the media id is a hash of the PATH, so ANY physical file move re-keys the item (db.metadata / db.progress / <id>.jpg thumbnail / transcodedPath(id) all keyed by id). A naive fs.rename+rescan = delete(old id, progress lost) + new-add(new id). Mitigation (chosen, justified): re-key + migrate db.metadata/db.progress and rename thumbnail/transcode/subtitle sidecars from getMediaId(oldPath) to getMediaId(newPath) atomically INSIDE one updateDatabase mutator, so the next scan takes the reuse fast-path with history intact. Dedup archive is unaffected (keys by extractor+id, not path). Applies to BOTH C1 (move files) and B2 Phase 2 (physical move of one-offs into the channel folder). First-class in the C1 design + a REQUIRED regression test: watch progress for a moved item survives the next scan unchanged (exec-plan C1/B2 ACs already lock this).

seven_answers.1_FR1_dual_validator: Two validators in lib/ytdlp/url.js; validateChannelUrl/classifySingleVideo UNCHANGED (subscriptions + YouTube one-offs). New validateOneOffUrl + classifyOneOffTarget; classifyOneOffTarget runs validateChannelUrl first (YouTube -> existing strict path verbatim), else validateOneOffUrl (non-YouTube -> pass user URL through verbatim, no reconstruction). Open host with a POSITIVE hostname-shape check (>=1 dot, ASCII labels, no userinfo) + all injection guards reused; one deliberate widening: FORBIDDEN_CHARS_ONEOFF permits URI-structural & = ? # (inert in an arg array; no shell ever). SF5 N/A (no reconstruction/interpolation). Shorts one-off recognized in classifySingleVideo without touching SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER. args.buildYtdlpDownloadArgs gains opts.targetUrl (re-validated, --no-playlist) for non-YouTube; subscriptions byte-identical.
seven_answers.2_FR4_move_trigger: Virtual grouping (Phase 1, always-on): GET /api/videos query-time union of root==channelDir OR db.metadata.channelUrl==sub.channelUrl, dedup by id, read-only, ships first. Physical move (Phase 2): trigger = ON SUBSCRIBE action (fire-and-forget after persist), NOT per-poll, NOT manual button; reuses C1 move machinery; only items already under the download root are eligible; idempotent (skip if already under channelDir); dedup archive untouched; scan-safe via the id re-key.
seven_answers.3_FR3_FR7_attribution: Parse-and-correlate (NOT per-URL spawns). New pure lib/ytdlp/failures.js parseItemFailureLine(line, knownIds) recognizes yt-dlp ERROR: [extractor] <id>: <reason>, attributes only when id in knownIds (else unattributed entry, never misattributed/dropped). run.spawnYtdlpDownload gains opts.knownIds + bounded itemFailures[] (same posture/cap as channelMeta[]; SF1 redact, SF3 bounded). runSubscriptionCycle maps failures onto activity LiveEntry {failures:[{videoId,title?,reason}]} via shallow-merge (no activity.js change); GET /api/subscriptions/status returns it verbatim; UI shows {channel,video,reason}. Phase (b) root-cause fixes are follow-on sub-tasks once reasons are visible.
seven_answers.4_subtitles: Grab: fixed-literal --write-subs --write-auto-subs --sub-langs en.* --sub-format vtt --convert-subs vtt (bundled ffmpeg) -> NO converter/dep for downloads. Local .srt: tiny pure srtToVtt in new lib/subtitles.js (no dep). Scan sets additive hasSubtitles (no re-processing). Serve: GET /api/subtitles/:id in server.js (works with yt-dlp disabled, path-confined, on-the-fly srt->vtt). Render (ONE approved player exception): <track kind=captions> on the persistent host + #cc-btn wired once like #pip-btn/#speed-btn; track rides reparented host; visibility gated on hasSubtitles only (no 2nd mobile signal); four-shell parity where control markup is duplicated.
seven_answers.5_FR8_DnD: Subscriptions management list in lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html (NOT the pinned sidebar). New persisted order field on the subscription record, backfilled by array index in ensureYtdlp (mirrors paused/skipShorts). New pure reduceReorder(subs, orderedIds) + reorderSubscriptions mutator + POST /api/subscriptions/reorder. Client reuses common.js moveArrayItem/computeDropIndex verbatim (mirrors folder-dnd test).
seven_answers.6_stats: Live compute from db.metadata per request (O(n), always fresh; cached aggregate rejected). GET /api/stats + pure lib/stats.js helpers unit-tested on a fixture. Most-watched: minimal net-new db.metadata.viewCount incremented ONCE per watch-page open via a dedicated POST /api/videos/:id/view (not per progress-POST/serve which over-count). Most-watched is the explicit drop-candidate if the round shrinks.
seven_answers.7_release_date: yt-dlp: extend CHANNEL_META_PRINT_TEMPLATE .{...}j with upload_date/release_date (fixed-literal, JSON-escaped, validated/bounded) -> downloadMeta bridge -> db.metadata.releaseDate at scan (Wave 3). Local: embedded ffprobe date (from the probe scan already runs) -> mtime fallback (fragile, honest last resort) (Wave 1). Schema-only additive backfill from data the scan already has; NO re-probe/re-thumbnail pass (backfill-regression lesson). sortItems gains release-date case (fallback addedAt); existing cases byte-identical; default regression-locked; AVAILABLE sort only (decision 8).

pe_design.waves: 7 collision-free waves, T1-T19, with a hot-file serialization proof table in the exec plan Design section. Chronic-hot files (lib/ytdlp/index.js in W2/3/5/6/7, server.js in W1/3/7, common.js in W1/2, style.css W1, player.js W4/5 only, store.js W2/3, args.js W3/5/6, url.js W6-isolated, four shells W1) each have a single owner per wave with strict serialize-after markers where two tasks in a wave both need one (W3 T9->T10 server.js, W5 T14->T15 index.js, W7 T18->T19 index.js, W4 T12->T13 player.js). New files lib/stats.js, lib/subtitles.js, lib/ytdlp/failures.js are collision-free by construction.

branch: feature/v1.24-ux-round (suggested; off main at v1.23.10). Coordinator owns ALL git.
base: main at v1.23.10 (a long v1.22.x/v1.23.x player + polish history just shipped). Prior shipped record thread in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (v1.22.x player parity/fixes, v1.23.x polish run).
scope_summary: LARGE, deliberately one branch. Combines the 9 coordinator-briefed FRs with the remaining ROADMAP.md Planned UX items into coherent DOMAIN GROUPS (downloads/yt-dlp, subscriptions, library/discovery, player/mobile UX, mobile polish, visual polish, mock-comments). PM to organize/de-dup into FR groups and propose a sensible BUILD ORDER (quick wins -> heavier/security-sensitive -> data-architecture) staying ONE branch by default; if genuinely too big for one healthy branch, PM/EM SAY SO and propose a phased split for Dean. Gate the whole execution list on Dean's approval before implementation. Flag every scope/clarification question for Dean rather than assuming.

A_downloads_ytdlp.note: Security-sensitive cluster -- widens/touches the yt-dlp spawn guard. NO shell:true ever; preserve arg-array spawns, `--` separator, host allowlist, --match-filter/SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER, SF4 path confinement, decoded-id charset (SF5). Disabled-yt-dlp-module no-op guarantee must hold (FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off -> no new routes/UI/DOM reachable).
items: FR-1 (briefed, HIGHEST security): process more than YouTube URLs -- broaden validateChannelUrl host allowlist / URL acceptance to other yt-dlp-supported sites, following yt-dlp extractor patterns. PM/PE decide: explicit expanded allowlist vs yt-dlp's own extractor list; and whether subscriptions or only one-off downloads get multi-site. Heaviest two-reviewer/adversarial gate of the round.

ROADMAP: accept share-sheet URL params (?si=/?is=) in the one-shot download API (page accepts, API rejects -- validateChannelUrl/FORBIDDEN_CHARS stricter than UI). Normalize/strip tracking params before validation WITHOUT weakening the guard. May cut the exit-code-1 spike. Two-reviewer gate.

ROADMAP: Shorts URL (/shorts/<id>) recognized for EXPLICIT one-off download in classifySingleVideo/validateChannelUrl -- must NOT undo the per-subscription skip-Shorts poll filter (SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER).

FR-3 (briefed): clearer download notifications -- show which channel/video, state, progress (percent/speed/ETA/per-item). FOLDS IN ROADMAP 'Clearer download progress' + 'Make yt-dlp errors visible' (surface real exit-code-1 reason: bad URL/members-only/geo/format/network).
ROADMAP: cancel an in-progress download from the main page (home status chip) -- cancel endpoint + process-handle tracking; cleanly kill the arg-array spawn (no orphan), mark cancelled (not failed).

FR-5 (briefed): clarify existing-channel pull timing -- FIRST document current polling behavior (FILETUBE_YTDLP_POLL_MINUTES, poll cycle, max-videos window) and flag it back to Dean, THEN propose surfacing last-pulled/next-pull/'checking now' + what's fetched in the UI.

FR-7 (briefed): investigate the 10-15 constantly-failing downloads -- root cause (Shorts rejected / members-only w/o cookies / geo/format / rate-limit / exit-code-1 spike). Pairs with FR-3. May need Dean to share a failing example.
ROADMAP: 'Re-pull all channels' prominence -- one clear 'check all subscriptions now' button, higher up / easy to reach.

ROADMAP (PM-TO-ADVISE): subtitle grab + closed captions. The GRAB side (yt-dlp --write-subs/--write-auto-subs, sidecar .vtt/.srt, local sidecar pickup) is in-domain; the CC RENDER side adds a <track> + CC button to the player CONTROL BAR, which leans OUT (player controls excluded). PM proposes download/grab-only, or defers the whole item -- advise, do not assume.

B_subscriptions.note: Reuse the gated subs + pins store (lib/ytdlp/store.js), subscriptions client/views, pins store/routes (/api/subscriptions/pins). NEVER write db.folders. Disabled-module no-op guarantee holds.
items: FR-2 (briefed, AMBIGUOUS -- flag for Dean): per-subscription action button in the .section-actions row (next to Shuffle/Rescan, public/index.html ~L108) in the channel/folder view. What does it DO? candidates: re-pull just this channel now / open this subscription's settings / unsubscribe / pin it. PM to propose + confirm with Dean.

FR-4 (briefed, MEATY data/architecture): reconcile pre-subscription one-off downloads into the channel. Videos downloaded one-off THEN subscribed live physically in the synthetic One-Off/Downloads folder but are tagged with channel identity (v1.22.0 FR-2 re-association); clicking the channel (/?root=<channelDir>) doesn't include them. Design approach: virtual grouping by channelUrl vs physical file move (auto on subscribe) -- interaction with scan, dedup archive, watch-progress. Two-reviewer gate.
FR-6 (briefed, small): pin a channel from a video's watch page (and/or subscriptions page) in one tap -- reuse the gated pins store + routes, NEVER db.folders.

FR-8 (briefed): drag-and-drop reorder subscribed channels (subs list / left sidebar) into an arbitrary persisted order. Mirror the existing folder DnD (v1.14.0: common.js moveArrayItem/computeDropIndex/rebuildFullFolderOrder, test/unit/folder-dnd-reorder.test.js). Store order in the subscriptions store.

ROADMAP: move files between folders -- move endpoint (fs.rename within mounted volumes; copy+delete fallback across devices) updating db.metadata filePath + thumbnail/transcode/progress keys; fail-safe path confinement (same discipline as delete + yt-dlp paths); must not look like delete+new-add to the next scan (preserve watch progress). UI: per-item 'Move to...' picker.
ROADMAP: show item count per view/folder/playlist -- 'N items' in the section header.
ROADMAP: format toggle -- videos only / audio only / both (item type already known per file; client-side filter + persisted preference).
ROADMAP: fun stats page -- counts + total duration + total size, breakdowns by folder/channel/type, longest/shortest/newest, maybe most-watched; retro-dashboard on-brand.

ROADMAP: capture release/upload date -> make it the DEFAULT home sort (yt-dlp upload_date/release_date at download; local files embedded date or mtime fallback), stored on db.metadata + a 'release date' sort. NOTE: making it the DEFAULT changes everyone's default ordering -- CONFIRM with Dean.

ROADMAP: use the YouTube channel's icon as its folder icon -- fetch+store channel avatar (yt-dlp channel thumbnail) alongside channel identity; render in place of the generic folder glyph everywhere (sidebar, playlists). Ties into the generated-avatar visual-polish item.

D_player_mobile_ux.note: Player CONTROLS are EXCLUDED (just finished a long player-polish streak). These three are player-adjacent UX (nav flash / resume-prompt behavior), NOT the control bar. Reuse the persistent single <video> host, dock FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED model, shared resolveMobileFormFactor/isMobileFormFactor. Confirm no player-controls scope creep.
items: ROADMAP: player flash on Prev/Next (~1/4s blank) -- keep the persistent <video> host continuously visible across SPA prev/next; investigate the watch-view re-render tear-down/re-mount or frame-clear-before-poster.
ROADMAP: skip the resume prompt for short saved progress (+ a Setting) -- shouldShowResumeOverlay prompts at >5s; raise threshold (skip under ~60s) and expose as a Settings option.
ROADMAP: resume prompt too small in the docked MiniPlayer -- suppress while DOCKED and only show in FULL / auto-resume in mini / expand to FULL when a resume decision is needed.

ROADMAP: subscriptions-page base zoom (IF it persists after v1.23.0 touch-action:manipulation) -- a horizontal-overflow child wider than viewport; needs on-device inspection (same family as v1.17.0/v1.15.0 min-width:auto overflow fixes). Low priority.

ROADMAP (CLARIFY): 'Playlists' label clarify -- the bottom-nav Playlists button ALREADY renders a 'Playlists' text label under its icon. Confirm with Dean whether he meant that control or a different affordance before changing anything.
ROADMAP: subscriptions page slightly too large on mobile (NOT zoom) -- tighten mobile padding/font/row sizing on the subs view.

ROADMAP + FR (briefed folds in): favicon still not showing on all browsers -- v1.22.2 added PNG rel=icon fallbacks but some browsers still miss it; add a real multi-res favicon.ico + re-check the full apple-touch-icon/sizes/shortcut-icon set across all four shells (four-shell parity discipline: index.html, watch.html, setup.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html).

ROADMAP: consistent user/uploader avatar icons -- replace the first-letter-uppercased avatar with a deterministic generated icon (identicon / stable color+glyph derived from channel name) so the same channel always shows the same avatar. Real avatar when available (ties into channel-folder-icon item C), generated fallback. Reuse era-theme tokens.
ROADMAP: more elegant buttons (less blocky) -- subtler bevels/rounding, tighter spacing+typography, cleaner hover; a polish pass keeping the era-theme system + 2000s character, NOT a redesign.

FR-9 (briefed, fun/low-risk): add 'Zak Goldin' mock-commenter persona to the retro mock-comments system -- 87% polite, 10% unhinged, 3% conspiracy-theory-about-whatever-the-video-is (weighted comment pool). Deterministic/tasteful. SEAM CORRECTION (grounded): the mock-comment pool is the commentBank array in public/js/watch.js getMockInitialComments() (~L864), NOT common.js; common.js holds the adjacent getMockViews()/getMockSubCount() (~L1052/1062) + getCommentCount() (~L819). Confirm the tasteful-weighting seam with the PM.

out_of_scope: Video PLAYER CONTROLS -- the 'Optional mobile control style (custom vs native iOS)' roadmap item stays OUT (just finished a long player-polish streak). The subtitle CC player-button rendering leans OUT if it means touching player controls.
Background audio for VIDEO (keep playing on lock/app-switch) -- exploratory / iOS-fragile; stays on the roadmap, not this round.
Ebooks + TTS -- parked on the roadmap.
Multi-user + permission-gated deletion (Accounts & security) -- deliberate separate foundational project.
Testing/infra roadmap section + Tech-debt tracker items -- OUT as scoped work (tests still accompany each FR as normal per CONTRIBUTING.md).
hard_constraints: PLAN-GATED: produce the full grounded execution list (Discovery -> Design -> tasks/exec plan with build order in collision-free waves) FIRST; NOTHING is implemented until Dean approves the plan.
ONE branch by default (feature/v1.24-ux-round). If genuinely too big for one healthy branch, PM/EM SAY SO and propose a phased split for Dean to decide -- do not silently split.

Flag EVERY scope/clarification question for Dean rather than assuming (FR-1 site-scope, FR-2 button action, FR-4 approach, FR-7 failing example, subtitles fit, 'Playlists' label meaning, release-date-as-default-sort, and any others surfaced during grounding).

Security core (FR-1/share-params/Shorts/cancel/FR-7): NO shell:true ever; preserve arg-array spawns, the `--` separator, the host allowlist, --match-filter/SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER, SF4 path confinement, and the decoded-id charset (SF5). Widening the allowlist must NOT weaken injection safety. Heaviest two-reviewer/adversarial gate on FR-1.
Disabled-yt-dlp-module no-op guarantee must hold for EVERY yt-dlp FR: FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off -> no new routes / UI / DOM reachable.
NEVER write db.folders from the subscriptions/pins paths -- reuse the gated subs + pins store.

Four-shell parity discipline for any shared-shell change (favicon, avatars, any new control): byte-identical across public/index.html, public/watch.html, public/setup.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html.
Reuse the SINGLE shared mobile-detection helper (resolveMobileFormFactor/isMobileFormFactor) -- never a second divergent 'is this mobile' signal.

File-move / folder-move must stay inside the configured/allowed mounts (never escape the sandbox -- same confinement as delete + yt-dlp paths) and must not make a moved file look like delete+new-add that loses watch progress.

Follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: Node 22 LTS, CommonJS, vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes, textContent over innerHTML, node:test for every new pure helper + a regression lock, lint 0 warnings (baseline 8 pre-existing common.js warnings), NO new runtime dependencies.

Node 22 toolchain at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin (prepend to PATH). npm test (1735 currently green), npm run lint (0 errors). SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint, never commit; coordinator owns ALL git.
clarification_questions_for_dean: FR-1 site-scope: WHICH sites should multi-site cover, and how -- an explicit expanded host allowlist (curated list) vs deferring to yt-dlp's own extractor list? And does multi-site apply to SUBSCRIPTIONS (polling) or ONLY one-off downloads?

FR-2 button action: what should the per-subscription action button (next to Shuffle/Rescan in the channel view) DO -- re-pull just this channel now / open this subscription's settings / unsubscribe / pin it?

FR-4 approach: reconcile pre-subscription one-off downloads by VIRTUAL grouping (channel view includes items tagged with that channelUrl regardless of physical folder) vs PHYSICALLY moving the files into the channel folder automatically on subscribe -- or both? (Affects scan/dedup/watch-progress.)
FR-7 failing example: can Dean share one or two of the 10-15 failing URLs (or a status/log snippet) so the root cause can be pinned down rather than guessed?

Subtitles/CC fit: OK to do the GRAB side only (download subs as sidecar/embedded) and DEFER the in-player CC toggle button (touches player controls, which are excluded) -- or defer the whole subtitles item to a later round?
'Playlists' label: which control did Dean mean? The bottom-nav Playlists button already shows a 'Playlists' text label -- is there a different affordance he wants labelled?

Release/upload date as DEFAULT sort: making captured release date the DEFAULT home ordering changes everyone's default feed order -- confirm that's wanted as the default (vs just adding it as an available sort option).

One-branch size: this is a LARGE round (~25 items across 7 domains). Coordinator's default is ONE branch -- confirm Dean is OK with one big branch, or does he want the PM/EM to propose a phased split by wave?
review_tier_proposed: Per-domain gating (finalized after PE Design). HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial gate on Group A yt-dlp security (FR-1 multi-site allowlist widening above all; share-param normalization; Shorts one-off; cancel endpoint/process-handle) and Group B FR-4 data-architecture (reconcile). FOCUSED two-reviewer on Group C move-files (path confinement + scan interaction) and release-date capture/default-sort. LIGHT single-QA on mobile/visual polish (Groups E/F), the resume-prompt/prev-next-flash player-adjacent items (Group D, with a player-non-regression check), and FR-9 mock-comments. Four-shell parity check wherever a shared shell changes (favicon, avatars). Dean's iPhone on-device pass remains the ultimate arbiter for mobile feel.

grounding_pointers.creator_reassociation_FR4: docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-08-v1.22-player-parity.md (FR-2), lib/ytdlp/index.js matchChannelDirToSubscription/backfillChannelIdentityFromFolder, server.js Phase-2 scan mutator, resolveChannelDir; one-off downloads land in the download root's flat/synthetic folder.

grounding_pointers.favicon_shells: all four shells' icon <link> tags (lines 7-9) currently SVG rel=icon + PNG rel=icon fallbacks (added v1.22.2) + apple-touch-icon + manifest; no favicon.ico. Assets in public/: favicon.svg, icons/icon-192.png, icons/icon-512.png, manifest.webmanifest.
grounding_pointers.overlapping_roadmap_folded: ROADMAP.md 'Clearer download progress' + 'Make yt-dlp errors visible' fold into FR-3; 'Shorts URL not recognized', 'Re-pull all channels', 'Move files between folders' pulled in as listed.
grounding_pointers.toolchain: Node 22 at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin (prepend PATH). npm test (1735 green), npm run lint (0 errors, 8 pre-existing common.js warnings baseline). Docker publish on tag push.

coordinator_decisions.note: Dean's 8 settled decisions, baked into the Discovery exec plan as fixed requirements (not forks). Recorded here so Design/Task-breakdown stages do not re-litigate them.
coordinator_decisions.1_multi_site_urls_FR1: One-off downloads accept ANY yt-dlp-supported site (broad). Subscriptions stay YouTube-only for phase 1 (validateChannelUrl/ALLOWED_HOSTS unchanged). The one-off path gets a NEW, separate, broader-but-still-injection-safe validator -- different validation for the two paths. Heaviest adversarial/two-reviewer gate; arg-array spawns, `--` separator, SF4 path confinement preserved; no shell:true ever.
coordinator_decisions.2_reconcile_one_offs_FR4: Do BOTH: virtual grouping by channelUrl (channel view shows every item tagged with that channelUrl regardless of physical folder) AND, on subscribe, physically move one-off files into the channel's folder (reuse move-files machinery). Sequence: virtual grouping first (safe), physical move second. Must be scan-safe + preserve watch progress; two-reviewer gate.
coordinator_decisions.3_subtitles_and_cc: Do it FULLY: grab subtitles (yt-dlp --write-subs/--write-auto-subs, .vtt/.srt, sidecar/embedded) AND add the in-player CC toggle (<track kind="captions"> + a CC button on the control bar). This is the ONE deliberate exception to the 'don't touch player controls' exclusion; do not otherwise redesign the player controls.
coordinator_decisions.4_delivery: ONE branch (feature/v1.24-ux-round), ONE big release (v1.24.0) at the end -- everything merges/ships together. Internally still planned as collision-free waves.
coordinator_decisions.5_per_subscription_button_FR2: The button next to Shuffle/Rescan (viewing a subscription's videos) = 'Re-pull this channel now' (on-demand poll of just that channel). Single action. Grounding: the backend endpoint (POST /api/subscriptions/:id/repull) already exists -- this is UI-only wiring.
coordinator_decisions.6_failing_downloads_FR7_plus_FR3: FR-7 and FR-3 MERGE: core fix is to capture yt-dlp's actual per-item stderr/failure reason and surface it (which channel + which video + why: bad URL, members-only, geo, format, rate-limit, etc.) in the download status UI. Scope as (a) capture+surface reasons, then (b) fix the common causes found.
coordinator_decisions.7_playlists_label_item: RESOLVED -- Dean meant the content-creator icon (avatar) next to the channel name, NOT the bottom-nav Playlists button (already labelled). DROPPED the Playlists-label item; folded intent into the avatar-icon work (F1): real channel icon when available (ties to channel-icon-as-folder-icon, C6), else a consistent deterministic generated avatar (not just the first letter).
coordinator_decisions.8_release_date_sort: Capture release/upload date and add it as an AVAILABLE sort option only -- do NOT make it the default (default home order unchanged).

tasks.title: Item count + format toggle + release-date sort case + deriveAvatar
Zak Goldin weighted comments + watch-page avatars
Subscriptions UI: repull + poll display + pin-from-watch + DnD client
Move files between folders + getMediaId id re-key
yt-dlp release-date + channel-avatar capture (fixed-literal print template)
Player prev/next flash fix (no reparenting-model change)
Resume threshold + Setting; docked resume-prompt decision
Cancel in-progress download (onChild hook + SIGKILL + cancelled state)
Subtitles grab + serve route + CC toggle (the one approved player exception)
FR-1 multi-site dual-validator + Shorts one-off + targetUrl plumbing
Physical move on subscribe (reuses T9 move machinery)
tasks.serialize_note: index.js: after T18; calls T9's exported moveItemToFolder

history.note: ENGINEERING-MANAGER opened the v1.24 'UX Round' -- a PLAN-GATED /kickoff-complex complex round, one branch (feature/v1.24-ux-round off main at v1.23.10). Coordinator briefed 9 UX FRs, then EXPANDED scope to also pull in ALL remaining ROADMAP.md Planned UX items (Player/mobile UX, Mobile polish, Downloads/yt-dlp, Library & discovery, Visual polish), making this a LARGE round -- intended, one branch. Grounded before routing: read ROADMAP.md (all Planned + Shipped context), lib/ytdlp/url.js (validateChannelUrl allowlist/FORBIDDEN_CHARS/classifySingleVideo -- the security core for FR-1/share-params/Shorts), lib/ytdlp/args.js (SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER/buildMatchFilterArg/arg-array spawn discipline), public/index.html .section-actions row (~L108, FR-2), and corrected the FR-9 mock-comment seam (the pool is watch.js getMockInitialComments() commentBank ~L864, NOT common.js; common.js holds getMockViews/getMockSubCount/getCommentCount nearby). Organized ~25 items into 7 domain groups (A downloads/yt-dlp, B subscriptions, C library/discovery, D player-adjacent mobile UX, E mobile polish, F visual polish, G mock-comments). Recorded OUT-of-scope (player controls incl. the custom-vs-native item; background audio for video; ebooks+TTS; multi-user/permission-gated deletion; testing/infra + tech-debt sections; the in-player CC toggle leans out). Captured 8 clarification questions for Dean to answer EARLY (FR-1 site-scope, FR-2 button action, FR-4 approach, FR-7 failing example, subtitles fit, 'Playlists' label meaning, release-date-as-default-sort, one-branch size / phased-split). Wrote the initial v1.24 feature-state (fr_groups, out_of_scope, hard_constraints, clarification_questions_for_dean, review_tier_proposed per-domain, grounding_pointers). No application/test code written; no git touched.

ENGINEERING-MANAGER set stage=discovery and wrote the product-manager Discovery inbox (.state/inbox/product-manager.md) with: the round framing (plan-gated, one branch, gate on Dean), the 7 domain groups + all ~25 items with grounded seams, the OUT-of-scope list, Dean's clarification questions to surface EARLY, the per-domain review-tier proposal, the security/no-op/parity constraints, and the deliverable (a Discovery exec plan grouping items into FR clusters with ACs, de-dup notes, and a PROPOSED BUILD ORDER in collision-free waves + a one-branch-health assessment / phased-split recommendation if needed). Instructed the PM to verify every item against live code before finalizing and to flag ALL ambiguities rather than assume. Routing to product-manager for Discovery; on completion coordinator runs /prep-pe-design. No application/test code written; no git touched.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery. Dean's 8 decisions (relayed by the coordinator) baked into the plan as settled requirements, not forks -- recorded verbatim in coordinator_decisions above. Wrote docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-09-v1.24-ux-round.md: Goal/Scope organized into 7 domain clusters (A-G) with tight, testable, [UNIT]/[MANUAL]/[PROCESS]-tagged acceptance criteria per FR; explicit Out-of-scope (player controls except the CC button, background-audio-for-video, ebooks+TTS, multi-user/auth, the default-sort change, testing/tech-debt sections); Constraints (spawn-guard discipline, disabled-module no-op, db.folders boundary, four-shell parity, single mobile-detection seam, era-theme tokens, textContent, Node 22/no new deps, one-branch/one-release, scan-safety); a 7-wave collision-free BUILD ORDER (quick visual/library wins -> subscriptions -> library-data/channel-identity -> player-adjacent -> yt-dlp non-security -> FR-1 multi-site heaviest gate -> FR-4 reconcile last) with chronic-hot-file notes (lib/ytdlp/index.js touched in 5 of 7 waves); per-cluster review tiers; a one-branch-health assessment (honest 'this is a lot for one branch' read, per-wave build+QA passes recommended, de-scope candidates identified without recommending cutting anything up front); and a 7-item Flag-for-principal-engineer section (FR-1 dual-validator design, FR-4 virtual-grouping+physical-move-safety, FR-3/7 per-item stderr attribution, subtitles pipeline, DnD-subs-reorder scope, stats-page data source, release-date-capture-for-local-files). Grounded every FR against live code before writing ACs (lib/ytdlp/url.js validateChannelUrl, args.js arg builders, run.js spawn/stderr handling, index.js runSubscriptionCycle/runOneShot, store.js subscription+pin shape, common.js sort/DnD helpers, watch.js commentBank, four-shell favicon lines, server.js delete-route pattern). Three notable grounding findings recorded in the Decision log: (1) both per-channel and repull-all backend endpoints ALREADY EXIST (lib/ytdlp/index.js ~L1322/~L1329) -- FR-2 and the repull-all-prominence item are UI-only wiring, shrinking/de-risking those two items; (2) today's subscription download spawn targets multiple survivor URLs in ONE invocation with a single aggregate error/stderr -- confirms FR-3/FR-7's exact root cause and that per-item attribution is new machinery, not better display of existing data; (3) the mock-comment pool has no existing weighting mechanism, so FR-9's 87/10/3 split is new selection machinery. No new ambiguities surfaced beyond the 7 flagged design questions for the principal-engineer -- Dean's 8 decisions covered the scope-level forks as expected. No application/test code written; no git touched. Set stage=design-ready; artifacts.requirements and artifacts.exec_plan both point at the exec plan. Reporting back to the coordinator/engineering-manager.

PRINCIPAL-ENGINEER completed Design. Wrote the ## Design section into docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-09-v1.24-ux-round.md after grounding against live code (lib/ytdlp/url.js, args.js, run.js, store.js, index.js, activity.js; server.js delete route + scan merge + getMediaId; common.js DnD/sortItems; player.js resume/dock; watch.js commentBank/avatar; four-shell favicon). Resolved all 7 PE design questions (recorded in pe_design.seven_answers). CRITICAL HAZARD found and mitigated as a first-class part of the move design: getMediaId(filePath)=md5(path) means any physical move RE-KEYS the item (metadata/progress/thumbnail/transcode all id-keyed) -- so C1 move + B2 Phase-2 physical move must re-key + migrate db.metadata/db.progress + rename thumbnail/transcode/subtitle sidecars from getMediaId(oldPath) to getMediaId(newPath) atomically inside ONE updateDatabase mutator (next scan = reuse, not delete+add); dedup archive keys by extractor+id not path, unaffected; required regression test locks watch-progress survival across a scan. FR-1 dual-validator: validateChannelUrl UNCHANGED; new validateOneOffUrl (open host + positive hostname-shape check, all injection guards reused, one deliberate & = ? # widening that is inert in an arg array with no shell, SF5 N/A because no reconstruction), with the explicit security argument written for the adversarial gate. FR-3/7: parse-and-correlate yt-dlp ERROR lines against knownIds (new lib/ytdlp/failures.js) into a bounded itemFailures[] surfaced on the activity LiveEntry -- NOT per-URL spawns. Subtitles: request .vtt directly + --convert-subs (no dep) for downloads, tiny pure srtToVtt for local .srt, GET /api/subtitles/:id serve route in server.js, <track>+#cc-btn as the ONE approved player exception. DnD on the subscriptions.html list with a new persisted order field. Stats: live compute + minimal viewCount for most-watched. Release-date: extend the channel-meta print template for yt-dlp items, ffprobe-date->mtime for local, additive backfill with no re-processing, available-sort-only. Finalized 7 collision-free waves (T1-T19) with a hot-file serialization proof table (single owner per wave for every chronic-hot file + explicit serialize-after markers within a wave). Data-model changes all additive; API changes all additive (no signature broken). Updated feature-state stage=design-complete, artifacts.design set. No application/test code written; no git touched. Reporting back to the coordinator/engineering-manager for task breakdown.

ENGINEERING-MANAGER completed the Task-breakdown stage. Read the full ## Design (per-cluster mechanics, the 7 answers, the T1-T19 wave/serialization table, the getMediaId re-key mitigation), RELIABILITY.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md before routing. Wrote a ## Task breakdown section into the exec plan: finalized T1-T19 (id, cluster, FR(s), title, EXACT owned files, ACs, gate tier, depends_on, wave, serialize-after markers), per-wave disjointness proofs, an all-chronic-hot-file serialization proof, a cross-file frozen-contracts summary, and an EM-refinements note. VERIFIED the one-owner-per-hot-file-per-wave invariant: no two CONCURRENT same-wave tasks share ANY file. RESOLVED under-specified CLIENT-side ownership the PE table left open: (W2) assigned client/subscriptions.js + watch.js to T6, dropped common.js as a W2 edit (B4 reuses moveArrayItem/computeDropIndex verbatim), split B3/B4/A4 routes into T8 and the store into T7; (W3) moved the C6 folder-icon RENDER into W1 via the channelAvatarUrl-precedence seam in T3/T4 so W3's T11 only POPULATES the field (no W3 client edit), gave the C1 move-picker to T9's common.js, shipped C4 stats as new files, placed the C5 sortItems case in T3 (common.js) with capture in T5 (server.js) joined by the releaseDate epoch-ms contract, and had T9 export moveItemToFolder for T19; (W5) put the A2 status-chip render in T14's common.js (JS-only, reusing the existing chip element) and serialized T15's cancel button after T14, isolating T16's CC markup to the player-hosting shells. Serialize-after markers: W3 T9->T10 (server.js), W4 T12->T13 (player.js), W5 T14->T15 (run.js+index.js+common.js), W7 T18->T19 (index.js). Wrote 19 self-contained software-developer inbox files (.state/inbox/software-developer-T1.md .. -T19.md), each with scope + exact owned files + frozen cross-file contracts + ACs + gate tier + the standard footer (Node 22 toolchain path, npm test/lint baselines 1735 green + 0 errors + 8 pre-existing common.js warnings, disabled-module no-op for every yt-dlp FR, COORDINATOR owns ALL git / SDEs report files-changed + test/lint only, never commit). Gate tiers: HEAVIEST adversarial two-reviewer on T17 (A1 multi-site, W6, url.js/args.js isolated) + T19 (B2 Phase-2 physical move, W7); two-reviewer on T9 (C1 move re-key), T14 (A2 attribution), T18 (B2 Phase-1 virtual grouping); light-to-medium on T5/T11/T15/T16/T12/T13; light on the visual/library quick wins + Zak Goldin. FLAGGED for Dean: (1) the client-ownership refinements above; (2) gate-tier delta -- the PE per-cluster tiers put T5/T11/T15 at focused two-reviewer while the coordinator summary implied lighter; I set them light-to-medium and recommend keeping them there BUT making T5's no-reprocessing-backfill regression test a hard gate (thumbnail-backfill lesson). Set stage=implementation, current_wave=1, current_tasks=[T1,T2,T3,T4,T5], populated the tasks array + wave_plan. No application/test code written; no git touched. Ready to launch Wave 1.

ENGINEERING-MANAGER: Wave 1 (T1-T5) shipped as v1.24.0 (git: 2367c49 Release v1.24.0 UX Round Wave 1 + 866dd36 roadmap record). Marked T1-T5 status=done, moved to completed_tasks. KICKED OFF Wave 2 (Subscriptions, ships as its own incremental release v1.24.1) per Dean's green light. Re-verified the Wave 2 hot-file serialization/collision proof against the exec-plan ## Task breakdown (line ~1422): T6={public/index.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html, lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js, public/js/watch.js}, T7={lib/ytdlp/store.js}, T8={lib/ytdlp/index.js} are STRICTLY DISJOINT; public/js/common.js is REUSED VERBATIM (unedited) so needs no W2 owner. No two tasks share any file -> no collision on the shared working tree. Functional dependency chain: T7 (order field + reduceReorder/reorderSubscriptions exports) -> T8 (imports T7, adds POST /api/subscriptions/reorder + pin route + computeNextPollDue + poll-timing status fields, all inside the isEnabled gate) -> T6 (client wiring: A5 repull-all, B1 per-channel repull, A4 poll display, B3 pin-from-watch, B4 DnD reusing common.js moveArrayItem/computeDropIndex verbatim). Recommended run order = dependency order T7 -> T8 -> T6 so each downstream task tests against real upstream code (files disjoint, so concurrency is collision-safe, but sequential guarantees testability). Confirmed T6 owns the CLIENT wiring + any new DOM for the new subscriptions controls in its four owned files (the Wave 1 common.js-helper/CSS fast-follow gotcha does NOT recur here: B4 reuses existing helpers verbatim and the subscriptions styling lives in subscriptions.html/existing style.css, no new common.js helper introduced by W2). Inbox files software-developer-T6/-T7/-T8 confirmed present and complete. Wrote the Wave 2 gate inboxes: build-specialist.md (Node 22 build/test/lint verify) + quality-assurance.md (light-tier review, gated-store + disabled-module-no-op + db.folders-boundary focus). Set current_wave=2, current_tasks=[T6,T7,T8], added wave_status. No application/test code written; no git touched. STOP after the Wave 2 gate per coordinator instruction (do NOT proceed to Wave 3; coordinator ships v1.24.1 and pauses for Dean).

COORDINATOR ran Wave 3 (Library data) end-to-end and shipped it as v1.24.2. Kicked off after Dean's on-device pass of v1.24.1, which surfaced a bug: the v1.24.1 subscription drag-reorder didn't work (on desktop too) — root-caused to MISSING CSS (the T6 JS toggled sub-row-dragging/-drag-over-before/-after classes that were never added to style.css: the recurring client-file ownership trap). Folded that CSS fix into the front of Wave 3 per Dean. Ran T9 (move+re-key), T11 (yt-dlp date/avatar capture) concurrently on disjoint files, then T10 (stats, serialize-after T9 on server.js). Each SDE self-reported Node 22 test/lint but the coordinator re-ran INDEPENDENT build-verify. Wave surfaced the predicted client-ownership fast-follows (SDEs correctly STOPPED at file-ownership boundaries rather than expanding scope): move-trigger wiring (main.js/watch.js — the picker existed in common.js but nothing invoked it), viewCount-ping (watch.js — the /view route existed but nothing fired it), T11-completion (run.js parseChannelMetaLine surfaced only 5 of 8 fields + server.js/index.js avatar scan-bridge lines were missing — the capture was inert despite green tests because the T11 integration test mocked runDownload), and a C6 render hop (coordinator edit: pins never carried channelAvatarUrl, so captured avatars stayed invisible — added read-time /api/subscriptions/pins enrichment matched by channelDir, re-validated). Two-reviewer gate (quality-assurance + adversarial general-purpose, both probing T9 confinement/re-key + T11 injection/avatar-XSS): NO critical; both headline risks independently probe-verified SOUND. Findings: 1 HIGH (TOCTOU destination-overwrite race in moveItemToFolder — existsSync-check-then-rename could silently clobber an unrelated file under concurrency), 2 MEDIUM (watch-page move didn't stop the player before the id re-keyed → mid-playback 404; subscriptions-LIST page still shows letter-avatars — a pre-existing W1 F1 gap, Dean DEFERRED it), + LOW nits. Applied fixes via SDE: TOCTOU → atomic-exclusive write (linkSync / copyFileSync COPYFILE_EXCL, EEXIST→409); player.close() before move-navigate; sanitizeChannelAvatarUrl rejects userinfo; doc/comment fixes; + regression tests for two documented move failure branches. The SDE also caught a pre-existing STALE test (the EXDEV fallback test mocked renameSync, which the atomic rewrite no longer calls) and repointed it. Focused re-confirm: SendMessage to the SAME adversarial reviewer, which PROBED the fix against real FS primitives + the live function and returned RESOLVED (no clobber/dup reproduces; residual crash-window is self-healing dual-hardlink, not data loss — accepted+documented). Final: 2076 tests green, lint 0 errors (8 pre-existing common.js warnings). Logged deferred/low items: tech-debt #22 (concurrent move+delete orphan+history-loss, self-heals) + #23 (crash-window non-atomicity), and ROADMAP Planned (subscriptions-list avatar). Release: bumped package.json 1.24.1→1.24.2, updated ROADMAP Shipped + this state, release branch → release commit → merge --no-ff to main → tag v1.24.2 → push main+tag → poll Docker 'Publish Docker Image' CI. Deploy go-ahead is now STANDING (Dean: 'no need to ask for the go-ahead anymore. just do it. push it.') — recorded in coordinator auto-memory. Waves 4-7 remain; PAUSE for Dean's on-device pass of v1.24.2 before Wave 4.

COORDINATOR shipped v1.24.3 (Wave 3 follow-up) from Dean's v1.24.2 on-device pass. Two on-device items: (1) pinned yt-dlp channels didn't drag-reorder (only the subscriptions-management list got DnD in v1.24.1) -> added it, mirroring the proven subscription reorder field-for-field: pin `order` backfill in ensureYtdlp, order-gap-safe reduceAddPin (order = 1+max, not list.length), reducePinReorder (pure) + reorderPins mutator (touches only db.ytdlp.pins), gated POST /api/subscriptions/pins/reorder, client DnD on renderPinnedSidebar reusing moveArrayItem/computeDropIndex + the existing .sidebar-item drag CSS; refactored the read-time pin channelAvatarUrl enrichment into a shared enrichPinsWithChannelAvatar helper feeding both GET and reorder. (2) removed the per-card 'Move to...' button from the home grid (Dean prefers move only from the watch page) -> main.js + test update, watch-page move untouched. Right-sized the gate to ONE focused adversarial review (low risk: a close mirror of already-shipped code + a UI removal): SHIP verdict, no defects, invariants (disabled no-op, db.folders, order-gap, avatar-enrichment parity) probe-verified. 2092 tests green, lint clean. Deploy autonomous (standing go-ahead). Also BACKLOGGED two Dean on-device findings to ROADMAP for future waves (he said stick on plan): the 'Release date' sort not visibly working (likely pre-v1.24.2 items have no captured releaseDate; investigate sortItems + populate coverage + possible additive backfill) and yt-dlp display titles losing original emojis to filename sanitization (capture the true title from metadata for DISPLAY, keep filenames filesystem-safe). Also saved a project memory: Dean wants one-off downloads organized into per-channel folders (sharpens FR-4/W7) -- he chose to KEEP FR-4 at its planned Wave 7 slot (after W4/W5/W6), not pull it forward. Waves 4-7 remain; PAUSE for Dean's on-device pass of v1.24.3 before Wave 4.
